item.title: Camp Mystic files for bankruptcy almost a year after deadly Texas floods
item.description: The camp listed its debt as exceeding $10 million.

item.title: U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iran inspections will happen, at some point
item.description: After conflicting remarks from Iran and the U.S., the U.N. nuclear agency chief says Iranian sites will be inspected, but the timing is "not essential."

item.title: FBI, NYPD carry out searches tied to bribery investigation of police officials
item.description: The searches stemmed from an ongoing probe into the conduct of former NYPD Chief of Department Jeffrey Maddrey, a source told CBS News.

item.title: American boy attacked by shark in Bahamas while swimming with brother
item.description: The boy was on a tour of the Bahamas' Exuma Cays with his family when the attack occurred, the Royal Bahamas Police Force said.

item.title: What a tech stock selloff means for your investments
item.description: A tech stock sell-off has triggered some volatility in the stock market. Kelly O'Grady explains what it could mean for your investments, including retirement accounts.

item.title: Senate passes Iran war powers resolution in symbolic rebuke of Trump
item.description: President Trump is facing growing backlash over the Iran war. On Tuesday, the Senate voted to limit the president's war powers and some lawmakers, including Republicans, have questioned the U.S. deal with Iran. Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Gen. Chris Donahue set to retire, in latest departure by top military official
item.description: Gen. Chris Donahue had earned the ire of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, multiple sources told CBS News.

item.title: Wounded soldiers, families accuse Army of downplaying war injuries
item.description: Soldiers say the Army disregarded warnings about thin defenses and ignored requests for medical supplies. Now they question whether the Army is being transparent about their injuries.

item.title: Trump to meet with Senate Republicans amid tension over elections bill
item.description: President Trump is set to attend a lunch meeting with Senate Republicans to push for an elections bill that GOP leaders have said has no chance of passing.

item.title: Wounded soldiers, families accuse Pentagon of downplaying injuries from Iranian strike
item.description: Survivors and their families are accusing the Pentagon of downplaying the injuries service members suffered during the deadly Iranian drone strike in Kuwait on March 1. In an exclusive interview with CBS News' Jonah Kaplan, a wounded soldier said he "absolutely" believes the Army and the Pentagon have tried to downplay the incident.

item.title: Bill Gates told Congress that Jeffrey Epstein made "veiled threats" to expose his affairs
item.description: Newly released testimony from billionaire Bill Gates reveals what he told lawmakers about his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Gates testified that after he cut ties, Epstein made what Gates considered "veiled threats" to expose his extramarital affairs. Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Dozens drown in France amid sweltering heat wave in Europe
item.description: At least 40 people have drowned in France trying to beat the heat as extreme temperatures hit Europe. Leigh Kiniry has more.

item.title: Hail, flooding and large wildfires just some of dangerous weather hitting across the U.S.
item.description: Hail almost the size of grapefruits pounded the Texas Panhandle as torrential rain slammed parts of the state and Oklahoma. Meanwhile, at least five wildfires continue to burn in Utah. Rob Marciano reports.

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo makes history as first player to score in 6 World Cups
item.description: Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice on Tuesday, making him the first player ever to score goals in six World Cups. The final round of the tournament's group stage begins on Wednesday. Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: Telescope snaps most detailed photo yet of Milky Way's heart
item.description: Euclid is on a mission to chart one-third of the sky in the hopes of shedding light on the enduring mysteries of dark matter and dark energy.

item.title: Alibaba sues Pentagon over blacklist designation
item.description: Chinese tech giant Alibaba has filed a federal lawsuit against the Defense Department for designating it a military-linked firm.

item.title: What's driving Latino voters? "The economy, and the economy, and the economy."
item.description: Leading Latino political strategists are examining the races where Latinos could play an outsize role in the midterm elections, and believe their votes will be critical in a number of House races.

item.title: Trump-linked firm is lobbying for pardons and its first client already paid $500K
item.description: Mo Strategies, started by former Trump campaign and administration officials, recently expanded its practice into the lucrative world of pardon lobbying.

item.title: North Korean soldier crosses border in suspected defection, report says
item.description: A North Korean soldier has been taken into custody after crossing the Demilitarized Zone into South Korea in a suspected defection, the Yonhap news agency says.

item.title: Trump says Iran agreed to nuclear inspections, but Tehran denies claim
item.description: Iran insists there are no plans for inspections of its bombed nuclear sites, but Trump says Tehran "fully and completely" agreed to let inspectors return.

item.title: Peru right-wing presidential hopeful Fujimori appears poised to win runoff
item.description: Peru's right-wing presidential candidate Keiko Fujimori built what may be an unassailable lead as vote counting for the runoff election entered its final stages, official figures showed.

item.title: All 3 Mamdani-backed candidates projected to win NY primaries
item.description: All three candidates backed by NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani were projected to win their Democratic primaries Tuesday night.

item.title: Brad Lander defeats Rep. Goldman in NY-10 Democratic primary, CBS News projects
item.description: Brad Lander has defeated two-term incumbent Congressman Dan Goldman for the Democratic nomination in New York's 10th Congressional District.

item.title: Assemblyman Micah Lasher wins Democratic House primary in NY-12, CBS News projects
item.description: State Assemblyman Micah Lasher won the crowded primary race for New York's 12th Congressional District, CBS News projects.

item.title: Mamdani influences New York City's congressional primaries with 3 big wins
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani endorsed three left-of-center candidates in the congressional Democratic primaries, and all three are set to win, CBS News projects.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Cait Conley wins NY-17 Democratic primary, CBS News projects
item.description: Democrat Cait Conley will challenge Republican Rep. Mike Lawler in the battle for New York's 17th Congressional District, which encompasses many of New York City's northern suburbs.

item.title: Federal court allows ICE to expand expedited deportations nationwide
item.description: A federal appeals court has allowed the Trump administration to move forward with an effort to expand fast-track deportations throughout the U.S.

item.title: Senate passes landmark housing affordability bill after bipartisan breakthrough
item.description: The Senate passed a bill aimed at lowering housing costs on Monday after a major breakthrough and rare bipartisan consensus.

item.title: India eyes a risky Everest mission to recover the body of "Green Boots"
item.description: A Mount Everest veteran tells CBS News why retrieving "Green Boots," whose remains have become a grim waypoint for climbers, would be a perilous mission.
